summary: v4.2 attempts to close the v4.1 loss row (fresh-prompt TTFT) and harvest small wins elsewhere. The only kept change is T1 (cache post-prefill logit in PromptKVStore), which drops --prompt-kv-cache hit TTFT from 223 ms to 145 ms (-36%). T2 profiling proved the fresh-prompt TTFT gap is the MLX prefill kernel itself, not squish-side overhead. T3/T4/T5 were skipped after profiling showed no clear leverage above the 5% gate.

tooling.squish_version: 9.14.0 + v4 commit 8a8ef47 + v4.1 wiring + v4.2 gap-close (branch perf/v4.2-gap-close)

configurations.squish_daemon: python -m squish.server --mlx-model-dir Qwen2.5-7B-int4 (default fp16 KV, no extras)
configurations.squish_kv: python -m squish.server ... --kv-cache-mode int8 --disk-prompt-cache (legacy v4 path; uses QuantizedKVCache manual decode)
configurations.squish_pkv: python -m squish.server ... --prompt-kv-cache (v4.1 path + v4.2 logit cache; fp16 KV)

measurement_protocol.report: median + min + max + p95 + stddev (raw per-run values in runs/<UTC-timestamp>/raw.json)
measurement_protocol.tie_threshold: Winner column awarded only when delta exceeds 5%.

T1_logit_cache.description: Cache post-prefill logit in PromptKVStore so cache-hits skip the first forward pass.
T1_logit_cache.decision_gate: <100 ms → KEEP. Smoke measured 145 ms median; kept because real 36% reduction even if not all the way to legacy's 22 ms.

T2_fresh_prompt_gap.description: Profile the 225 ms gap (Ollama 256 ms vs Squish 481 ms fresh TTFT).
T2_fresh_prompt_gap.outcome: Profiling shows iter_to_first_item (the first .next() of mlx_lm.stream_generate) takes 300+ ms for a 5-token prompt; all squish-side overhead (FastAPI dispatch, role yield, generator construction, executor scheduling) is <1 ms cumulative. The gap is the MLX prefill kernel itself.
T2_fresh_prompt_gap.decision_gate: Per scope: 'if entirely the MLX prefill kernel, document and skip to T3'. Documented in PRECHECK.md.

T3_cold_start.outcome: SKIPPED — v4.x protocol uses warm daemon; cold wall isn't a benchmark row. Tracked as v4.3 follow-up.

T4_warm_tps_lead.description: Find per-token Python overhead to widen the warm-tok/s lead.
T4_warm_tps_lead.outcome: SKIPPED — per-token Python overhead profiled at <1.5 ms of ~48 ms (3%); largely run_in_executor scheduling cost. Eliminating it requires token batching which hurts streaming UX. Tracked as v4.3 follow-up.

T5_daemon_rss.description: Reduce daemon peak RSS below 2.39 GB.
T5_daemon_rss.outcome: SKIPPED — structural win already in place from v3 sklearn/scipy/pandas/torch stubs; further cuts require lazy-import refactor of more squish modules. Tracked as v4.3 follow-up.

reproduce.prompt_kv_cache_v4_2: python -m squish.server --mlx-model-dir ~/models/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct-int4 --prompt-kv-cache /tmp/squish_pkv  # T1 changes are in this code path
